observations.display: Dopamine transporter (DaTSCAN): significantly reduced uptake in putamen

observations: MRI: mild diffuse cerebral atrophy, normal T2 signal in basal ganglia
record.note: Patient presents with a 4-year history of progressive motor symptoms beginning in the right hand. DaTSCAN confirms dopaminergic deficit. Currently on optimized Levodopa therapy with good on-time but experiencing wearing-off effects. Non-motor symptoms include REM sleep behavior disorder, constipation, and anosmia — likely representing the prodromal phase that preceded motor onset. Family history is negative for Parkinson's disease. No cognitive impairment detected on MoCA (score 27/30). Blood pressure shows orthostatic drop of 20 mmHg systolic. Consider adjustment of dopaminergic therapy or addition of COMT inhibitor if wearing-off worsens.
